title: Rootless podman in podman: "error from newuidmap: newuidmap: open of uid_map failed: Permission denied"
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When running rootless podman inside a container, I get the errors:
```
time="2022-02-26T17:07:02Z" level=warning msg="\"/\" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers"
time="2022-02-26T17:07:02Z" level=debug msg="error from newuidmap: newuidmap: open of uid_map failed: Permission denied\n"
Error: exit status 1
cannot setup namespace using newuidmap
```

I have tried reinstalling `shadow-utils` as others have suggested, as well as messing with the perms on `/usr/bin/newgidmap`
neither has resolved the issue. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Building this container will fail on the last `podman system migrate` step:
```
FROM quay.io/centos/centos:stream9 

RUN dnf install -y podman fuse-overlayfs
RUN dnf reinstall -y shadow-utils
RUN useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash -d /home/testuser -u 1000 -m testuser
RUN sed -i -e 's|^#mount_program|mount_program|g' /etc/containers/storage.conf
RUN sed -i -e 's|^\[machine\]|#[machine]|g' /usr/share/containers/containers.conf
RUN getcap /usr/bin/newuidmap
RUN stat /usr/bin/newuidmap
RUN chmod 4755 /usr/bin/newgidmap
RUN chmod 4755 /usr/bin/newuidmap
USER testuser
RUN podman --log-level trace system migrate
```

**Describe the results you received:**

**Describe the results you expected:**

A container with podman usable as the `testuser`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
The user is listed in the `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` and the permissions are correct on `/usr/bin/newuidmap`

comments.body: Have you tried to run quay.io/podman/stable and does it work?

Does podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map work within the containerfile?

Did you populate the /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid files within the container?

What podman command are you using to run this container?

there was no feedback to the questions above, so I am going to close the issue.

Please reopen if it still persists and you provide the requested information

Oops, thanks, sorry -- yes -- I was able to crib from the podman container in fedora to make a Centos9-Stream container that was capable of running podman-in-podman (and ultimately make a jenkins worker container) -- the key seemed to be adding a second entry into subgid/subuid. When running the container I included in my first post, and reinstalling shadow-utils, it will only make 1 entry in the files for the user.. So by running:
```
RUN echo -e "jenkins:1:999\njenkins:1001:64535" >/etc/subuid
RUN echo -e "jenkins:1:999\njenkins:1001:64535" >/etc/subgid
```
It worked (in combination with the fuse-overlayfs setup/mount)

thanks for your help.
@DaveWK Could you please provide a complete workaround?
